enonce.texte:
Look at the pictures and write the name of each job.

Choose from the words in the box:

teacher doctor farmer shopkeeper nurse driver
images: A person standing in front of a classroom with a blackboard and students
A person wearing a white coat with a stethoscope around the neck
A person working on a farm with crops and tools
A person standing behind a counter in a shop with goods on shelves
A person in a white uniform with a medical cap, holding a clipboard
A person sitting in the driver's seat of a car or bus

questions.texte: Write the name of each job next to the correct number.
questions.reponse:
1. teacher
2. doctor
3. farmer
4. shopkeeper
5. nurse
6. driver

sousQuestions.texte: Which person works on a farm?
sousQuestions.reponse: A farmer works on a farm.

intitule: Exercise 2: Reading - What do they do?

enonce.texte:
Read the sentences and match each person with their job.

1. She helps sick people. She works in a hospital.

2. He teaches students. He works in a school.

3. She sells things in a shop.

4. He grows crops and raises animals.

5. He drives people from one place to another.

questions.texte: Match each sentence with the correct job from the list: teacher, doctor, farmer, shopkeeper, driver.

sousQuestions.reponse: A nurse helps sick people and works in a hospital or clinic.
A teacher teaches students in a school.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets.

1. A doctor ______ (help) sick people.

2. A teacher ______ (teach) students.

3. A farmer ______ (grow) crops.

4. A shopkeeper ______ (sell) things in a shop.

5. A driver ______ (drive) a car or a bus.

6. A nurse ______ (take) care of patients.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct verb form.
questions.reponse:
1. helps
2. teaches
3. grows
4. sells
5. drives
6. takes

sousQuestions.texte: Change the sentences to negative form.
sousQuestions.reponse:
1. A doctor does not help sick people. (or doesn't help)
2. A teacher does not teach students.
3. A farmer does not grow crops.
4. A shopkeeper does not sell things.
5. A driver does not drive a car.
6. A nurse does not take care of patients.

sousQuestions.texte: Change the sentences to interrogative form.
sousQuestions.reponse:
1. Does a doctor help sick people?
2. Does a teacher teach students?
3. Does a farmer grow crops?
4. Does a shopkeeper sell things?
5. Does a driver drive a car?
6. Does a nurse take care of patients?

enonce.texte:
Write the plural form of each job and pronounce it correctly.

Remember: /s/ for voiceless sounds, /z/ for voiced sounds, /ɪz/ for words ending in -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -z.

questions.texte: Write the plural form of these jobs: teacher, doctor, farmer, shopkeeper, nurse, driver.
questions.reponse: teachers, doctors, farmers, shopkeepers, nurses, drivers

sousQuestions.texte: How do you pronounce the plural of 'nurse'?
sousQuestions.reponse: Nurses is pronounced /ˈnɜːrsɪz/ because it ends with 'se' which sounds like /s/ and takes the /ɪz/ ending.

sousQuestions.texte: How do you pronounce the plural of 'teacher'?
sousQuestions.reponse: Teachers is pronounced /ˈtiːtʃərz/ because it ends with a voiced sound (/r/) and takes the /z/ ending.

intitule: Exercise 5: Reading - A Day in the Life

enonce.texte:
Read the text about Mr. Kofi and answer the questions.

My name is Mr. Kofi. I am a farmer. I live in a village near Buea. Every day, I wake up at 5:00 am. I go to my farm and work until 12:00 pm. I grow corn, cassava, and vegetables. I also have some chickens and goats. I sell my products at the local market every Saturday. I like my job because I work outside and I help people eat healthy food. My wife is a nurse. She works at the village health centre. She helps sick people every day. We have two children. They are students at the local school.
images: A farmer working in a field with crops and a hoe, with a village background

questions.texte: Read the text carefully and answer the questions.

sousQuestions.texte: What does he grow on his farm?
sousQuestions.reponse: He grows corn, cassava, and vegetables.

sousQuestions.texte: Where does Mr. Kofi sell his products?
sousQuestions.reponse: He sells his products at the local market.

sousQuestions.texte: Why does Mr. Kofi like his job? Give two reasons.
sousQuestions.reponse: He likes his job because he works outside and he helps people eat healthy food.

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph about your dream job.

Use these questions to help you:

  • What is your dream job?
  • What does this person do?
  • Where does this person work?
  • What tools or things does this person use?
  • Why do you want to do this job?
images: A child thinking about different professions with thought bubbles showing a doctor, teacher, pilot, and artist

questions.texte: Write 5-7 sentences about your dream job.
questions.reponse: Example: My dream job is to be a doctor. A doctor helps sick people. A doctor works in a hospital or clinic. A doctor uses a stethoscope and other medical tools. I want to be a doctor because I want to help people who are sick. I also want to save lives. I think it is a very important and noble job.

enonce.texte:
Work with a partner. Ask and answer questions about jobs.

Use these questions:

  • What does your father do?
  • What does your mother do?
  • What job do you want to do in the future?
  • Why do you want to do that job?

Then, write a short report about your partner's answers.
images: Two students sitting at a desk talking and writing, with speech bubbles showing 'What does your father do?' and 'He is a driver.'

questions.texte: After the interview, write a short report about your partner.
questions.reponse: Example: My partner's name is Amina. Her father is a driver. He drives a taxi in the city. Her mother is a shopkeeper. She sells clothes in the market. Amina wants to be a teacher in the future. She wants to be a teacher because she loves children and wants to help them learn.

enonce.texte:
Listen to the teacher read the descriptions and write the name of each job.

1. This person works in a hospital. He helps sick people. He uses a stethoscope.

2. This person works in a school. She helps students learn. She writes on the board.

3. This person works on a farm. He plants and harvests crops.

4. This person works in a shop. She sells things to customers.

5. This person works in a clinic. She takes care of patients. She wears a white uniform.

6. This person drives a bus or car. He takes people to different places.

questions.texte: Write the name of the job for each description.
questions.reponse:
1. doctor
2. teacher
3. farmer
4. shopkeeper
5. nurse
6. driver

enonce.texte: Match the jobs with the tools they use and the places they work.

questions.texte: Draw lines to match the job with the correct tool and place.

sousQuestions.reponse: A teacher uses a board and works in a school.
A doctor uses a stethoscope and works in a hospital.
A farmer uses a hoe and works on a farm.
A shopkeeper uses a cash register and works in a shop.
A nurse uses a thermometer and works in a clinic.
A driver uses a steering wheel and works in a car or bus.

intitule: Exercise 10: Writing - Describing a Family Member's Job

enonce.texte:
Write 4-5 sentences describing a job of a family member or someone you know.

Include:

  • Who they are
  • What their job is
  • What they do at work
  • Where they work
  • What tools they use
images: A simple family tree with labels showing different family members and their jobs

questions.texte: Write a short description of a family member's job.
questions.reponse: Example: My uncle is a driver. He drives a bus from Douala to Yaoundé. He wakes up very early every day. He takes many passengers to different cities. He uses a steering wheel and a gear stick. He likes his job because he meets many people.
